purpose: First editable site mesh layer generated from the user-validated V-scope, with source refs kept per selectable object.

validation_event.validated_scope: V01-V23 visible site 3D scope, including V12 driveway split and V13 terrace/path split
validation_event.note: User confirmed the visible parcel scope is correct and can be used as the base for the next source-linked site mesh step.

coordinate_system.axis: Mesh data stores source X/Y on ground plane and Z as display height; WebGL viewer remaps to Three.js X/Z ground plane with Y up

house_position_only.label: Woning positie placeholder, geen definitieve shell

tree_conifer.label: Spar placeholder from CAD tree symbol

tree_deciduous.label: Loofboom placeholder from CAD tree symbol

material.label: Woning positie placeholder, geen definitieve shell

objects.label: Perceelsgrens volgens blauwe PDF-lijn + CAD control

height_basis.scale_note: Height is scaled into the current CAD site source coordinate system; provided max height and 45 degree pitch drive the visible roof, while the 109 m3 volume is retained as a QA control value.

real_world_metric_source.description: Metrische gegevens van het bijgebouw/tuinhuis zoals door de gebruiker doorgegeven.
rules: CAD footprint and source_entity_ids remain the geometric source of truth for 2D placement.
These metrics override temporary display height for V04 only.
Use the user-confirmed 45 degree roof pitch and 3.77 m maximum height for the visible gable profile.
Keep the 109 m3 volume as a QA control value; with 3.57 m width, 45 degree pitch and 3.77 m ridge the simple exterior envelope volume is about 115.1 m3.
The current site-plan extraction uses source coordinates; display height is scaled from the validated V04 footprint until global site scale is recalibrated.
